query: mutation UpdateDiscussion($body: String!, $discussionId: ID!) { updateDiscussion(input: {discussionId: $discussionId, body: $body}) { discussion { url } } } }

variables.body:
## GIVER Index Pilot Report — 2025

*Posted by @EthanThatOneKid | April 2026*

---

## What We Built

The GIVER Index: an open standard measuring the circular vs. linear tilt of cultures.

**Core pipeline:** `giver-index compute --year 2025`

**5 feeds wired so far:**
- Hofstede 6-D (LTO, IVR) → Generative + Rooted
- World Bank GDP per capita → Impact
- WVS Inglehart → Evidence-based (partial join, ~52 countries)
- Top 200 people → People league scoring
- Countries GeoJSON → Regional clustering

**Outputs generated:**
- `giver_index_2025.csv` — 239 countries scored
- `giver_index_2025.geojson` — map overlay ready
- `giver_index_2025.json` — JSON export
- `export-mirofish` — full simulation context package

---

## Key Findings

### 1. The Hofstede data has real holes
Of 111 Hofstede countries, only **52 map cleanly** to ISO3. The CSV uses internal 3-letter codes that don't match ISO standards directly. Required hand-crafted mapping with ~100+ entries.

### 2. China / Russia / India / Brazil / Indonesia — no Hofstede data
These major economies score 0.0 on Generative and Rooted because their Hofstede codes don't exist in the CSV. Their scores come from GDP only. **This is the single biggest gap.**

### 3. WVS join was harder than expected
The official WVS requires formal registration. Found two useful proxies: a published academic dataset (~90 countries) and the EVS/WVS Trend File.

### 4. Tiny territories score 0.0 (correctly)
Liechtenstein, Vatican, North Korea, Syria, Cuba — no feeds, score 0.0. ~40 countries total.

### 5. Only 10 of 239 countries score as circular
Most countries score below 50 because Hofstede dimensions carry heavy weight and most countries have no Hofstede data. This will improve as more feeds are added.

---

## MiroFish Export (Two-League System)

People compete with people (Top 200), countries compete with countries (239). The Top 200 richest are almost all "linear" (US-domiciled, high individualism, low LTO). European-domiciled billionaires score slightly higher due to home-country LTO/IVR.


Only Luxembourg scores as **Giver Elite** (72.2/100).

## Current Scores (Top 5)
1. Luxembourg — 72.2
2. Macau — 65.5
3. Sweden — 62.1
4. Japan — 61.9
5. South Korea — 61.5

## Gaps Remaining
- China/Russia/India/Brazil/Indonesia: no Hofstede → need alt source or manual data entry
- WVS: formal registration required → consider Pew + academic proxies instead
- GitHub per-capita: no reliable public endpoint → consider World Bank or academic dataset
- Pew reincarnation data: web scraping required → scrape Pew table from their 2025 report

## Commits
See GitHub repo: https://github.com/EthanThatOneKid/giver-index
